task_completion.description: Import or open the 14-day aquarium chemistry data in a spreadsheet application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A spreadsheet application (e.g., LibreOffice Calc) is open, displaying 14 rows of water chemistry data including columns for dates, Ammonia, Nitrite, Nitrate, and pH.

task_completion.description: Calculate the parameter averages for the water chemistry data.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Cells at the bottom or side of the data containing calculated averages for Ammonia (~0.38), Nitrite (~0.55), Nitrate (47.0), and pH (~7.14).

task_completion.description: Apply conditional formatting to highlight dangerous readings.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Specific cells in the data range are visually highlighted (e.g., colored backgrounds or red text) corresponding to dangerous levels (Ammonia >0.25, Nitrite >0.5, Nitrate >40).

task_completion.description: Calculate and display the total violation count.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A specific cell or section explicitly labeled as total violations, showing a count (actual total is 34, or individual counts of 13, 9, and 12).

task_completion.description: Identify the primary water quality problem.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Text in the spreadsheet explicitly stating that 'Ammonia' is the primary water quality problem or the most frequent violation.
The spreadsheet application's title bar shows 'aquarium_analysis.xlsx' and/or a save dialog confirms the directory is /home/ga/Documents/.

integrity.description: The agent used a GUI spreadsheet application to perform the analysis.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with a spreadsheet GUI (like LibreOffice Calc) rather than just a terminal window running a Python/bash script to generate the file.

integrity.description: The agent applied actual formatting to the cells.
integrity.visual_evidence: The dangerous readings have visual formatting (like background colors or font colors) applied within the spreadsheet grid, rather than just adding a text column saying 'Dangerous'.

integrity.description: The agent calculated the values rather than hardcoding expected text.
integrity.visual_evidence: The spreadsheet shows formula bars being used for averages/counts, or the final numbers match the actual data (e.g., 34 total violations) rather than just copying text from a prompt.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The 14-day data spans 2024-01-01 to 2024-01-14. Actual averages: Ammonia ~0.38 ppm, Nitrite ~0.55 ppm, Nitrate 47.0 ppm, pH ~7.14. Dangerous thresholds: Ammonia > 0.25 ppm, Nitrite > 0.5 ppm, Nitrate > 40 ppm. Actual violation counts: Ammonia (13), Nitrite (9), Nitrate (12), Total = 34. The primary water quality problem is Ammonia (most frequent violations).
